questions.q: A smooth atlas on $M$ is a collection of charts $\{(U_\alpha,\varphi_\alpha)\}$ covering $M$ such that:
choices: the transition maps $\varphi_\beta\circ\varphi_\alpha^{-1}$ are $C^\infty$ diffeomorphisms between open subsets of $\mathbb{R}^n$
questions.explain: Smoothness of transition maps between Euclidean patches is exactly what lifts calculus from $\mathbb{R}^n$ to $M$. No disjointness, compactness, or linearity is required.

questions.q: On $S^2$ with the two-chart stereographic atlas from $N$ and $S$, the overlap $U_N\cap U_S$ is $S^2$ minus how many points?
questions.explain: The north chart misses $N$ and the south chart misses $S$, so the overlap is $S^2\setminus\{N,S\}$ â€” two missing points.

questions.q: The transition map between the two stereographic charts on $S^2$ is:
questions.explain: Composing the two stereographic projections gives $(u,v)\mapsto(u,v)/(u^2+v^2)$, a $C^\infty$ diffeomorphism of $\mathbb{R}^2\setminus\{0\}$.

questions.q: For a smooth $n$-manifold $M$ and $p\in M$, the tangent space $T_pM$ is:
choices: the tangent line to a fixed curve through $p$
the kernel of some linear map on $M$
questions.explain: In any chart the coordinate vectors $\partial/\partial x^1,\dots,\partial/\partial x^n$ form a basis, so $T_pM$ is an $n$-dimensional real vector space.

questions.q: What is the dimension of the tangent bundle $TM$ of a smooth $n$-manifold $M$?

Under a chart change $y=y(x)$, a tangent vector with components $X^i$ becomes $\tilde X^j = (\partial y^j/\partial x^i)X^i$. If $X=(2,0)$ in coordinates $x$ and the Jacobian at the point is $\begin{pmatrix}3&1\\0&2\end{pmatrix}$, what is $\tilde X^1$?
A smooth map $f\colon M^m\to N^n$ is an immersion at $p$ iff $df_p$:
questions.explain: Immersion = injective differential, rank $m$. Submersion is the surjective case with rank $n$.

questions.q: By the regular value theorem, if $c$ is a regular value of $g\colon\mathbb{R}^{n+1}\to\mathbb{R}$ then $g^{-1}(c)$ is a smooth manifold of dimension $n$. For $g(x)=\|x\|^2$ on $\mathbb{R}^4$ and $c=1$, what is the dimension of $g^{-1}(1)=S^3$?
questions.explain: $g\colon\mathbb{R}^4\to\mathbb{R}$, and $1$ is regular, so $g^{-1}(1)$ has dimension $4-1=3$.

questions.q: Whitney's embedding theorem states that every smooth $n$-manifold admits a smooth embedding into:
questions.explain: Whitney (1944): every smooth $n$-manifold embeds smoothly in $\mathbb{R}^{2n}$, and immerses in $\mathbb{R}^{2n-1}$.
